notes: Generally runs fine but usually lags when loading up a map and the game will lags more on a larger map. The mouse move towards the lower right part of the screen when the physical mouse is moved VERY SLOWLY so it is still playable
title: VRChat

notes:
I had to add the following launch option to start directly in the hub to prevent crashing on startup:
vrchat://launch?id=wrld_b51f016d-1073-4c75-930d-9f44222c7fc3:7085
Other than that, it'll often go down to 45FPS on crowded worlds and it hangs when trying to load some worlds.
title: VRChat

notes: I ran on desktop mode only. It runs and plays well. Only issues are with worlds/avatars having certain shaders that does not load. But most worlds/avatars can be seen. Some worlds crash/hang after couple seconds or won't load. I have streamed my experiences on Twitch.tv and so far have one vid on YouTube. When I first started playing under Wine back in July 2018, some avatars I couldn't see but I can now see. Things are slowly improving over time. But some worlds that I was able to go to I can't now, due to updating shaders. But experience depends on your systems setup, what doesn't work for me could work for you. Also note, default home does not work for me. Had to go to Hub after tutorial and set as home. I have 233 hours play time.
title: VRChat

notes:
What works:
Desktop- and VR-mode
a good amount of avatars
all worlds i have tested (only 4)
audio (microphone as well)
gamepad

What doesn't work:
Some avatars are not displayed correctly. The problem is somehow linked to the avatars, but it isn't just the shader used as the same shader works on other avatars.
Crashes when joining certain instances (other instances of the same world work)
 This might also be linked to a bug in VRChat that does the same thing in a lot less frequently.

Tweaks:
Needed to use startoption "-show-screen-selector" and set graphics mode to DesktopLow to not crash after certain amount of time.

Performance:
The performance is really good. I wasn't able to see any worse performance that on windows. If anything the performance was better.
title: VRChat

notes:
What works: Desktop- and VR-mode; all worlds and avatars I have tested; audio (microphone as well) gamepad 
   What doesn't work:  Crashes when joining certain instances (other instances of the same world work) This might also be linked to a bug in VRChat that does the same thing in a lot less frequently; 
Frequent crashed when using a VR-Headset
   Tweaks: On Nvidia cards, a fairly new driver is needed. Version 399 had a lot of things not render and crashed frequently. Version 415 renders everything for me and only crashes when using VR. 
   Performance: The performance is really good. I wasn't able to see any worse performance that on windows. If anything the performance was better.

notes: Tested in Desktop/Non-VR mode on VRChat 2019.1.2. Crashes when loading certain avatars and worlds. Voice chat sometimes gets stuck and loops over the same few seconds. title: VRChat

notes: Launched fine, but had constant spinning after entering actual gameplay.
title: VRChat

notes: The issue of crashing seems to come up with any world that uses the built in video player youtube-dl.exe. I have solved the crashing by renaming steam/steamapps/common/VRChat/VRchat_Data/StreamingAssets/youtube-dl.exe to youtube-dl.exe-do-not-use effectively disabling it entirely. This comes with the issue of video players in game only displaying black instead of a video but this will make it so you can play in these worlds. Everything else seems to work perfectly no crashing even after hours of game play and all avatars seem to load properly.
title: VRChat

title: VRChat

notes: Works perfectly in VR mode after simply renaming the youtube-dl.exe to anything else in the game directory/VRChat_Data/StreamingAssets. This will break video playback in game but remove pretty much all intermittent crashing.
title: VRChat

notes: Works perfectly in 2D after renaming steam/steamapps/common/VRChat/VRchat_Data/StreamingAssets/youtube-dl.exe to anything else. This will stop the game from attempting to play videos in game, which currently causes crashing. Have not tested in VR yet. Game crashes for me when attempting to exit the game, but that's not a large problem.
title: VRChat

notes: Works great in 2D after renaming steam/steamapps/common/VRChat/VRchat_Data/StreamingAssets/youtube-dl.exe to anything else. This will stop the game from attempting to play videos in game, which currently causes crashing. If your mouse rotation is messed up in 2D play, alt-tab out of the game and then click the game window to resume playing, and that fixes the issue 9 times out of 10. VR works great but is usually around 45 FPS for me. Not sure why VR does not go to 90 FPS because my system wasn't maxing out any resources. Game crashes for me when attempting to exit the game, but that's not a large problem.
title: VRChat

cpu: AMD A8-6500 APU with Radeon HD Graphics
notes: Tested in Desktop/Non-VR mode on VRChat 2019.2.1. Crashes when loading certain avatars and worlds. Voice chat sometimes gets stuck and loops over the same few seconds. notes: I tested the game in desktop mode. It is playable, but you should expect crashes every so often. I'm not sure that I can outright confirm it, but I have a lurking suspicion that certain avatars are behind the crashes. I crashed several times in a row attempting to return to a specific instance of a world with the same people in it, but entering a new instance of that same world did not crash my game. I will retest the game in VR when I am able and likely send in another report based upon my results with that.

notes:
VRChat is working after disabling Esync with the launch options:
PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command%

It laggs sometimes and crashing very rarely, but that's not a problem of Proton.

title: VRChat

notes: Needs Vulkan development drivers on Nvidia to not crash randomly (https://developer.nvidia.com/vulkan-driver). Also steam/steamapps/common/VRChat/VRchat_Data/StreamingAssets/youtube-dl.exe needs to be replaced with an empty file, or VRChat will crash when trying to load a video player. This file gets overwritten on every update even when it is write protected. ¯\_(ツ)_/¯ Performance is almost as good as on windows. Works fine in VR.
title: VRChat

notes: Works very fine, but certain maps really just give you a black screen and freeze.

notes: It runs fine, but struggles with heavy memory leaks which do not happen on Windows. The eSync fix suggested earlier seems to work fine.

notes:
While the game ran and played without issue I could not get my microphone to work on it.

I tested the microphone to ensure it was plugged in and working (which it was) but my options ingame were either "pulse audio" or "pulse audio (default)" neither of which enabled me to talk to others.

It is because of this issue I'm giving it Broze and not Silver or Gold as talking in this game is pretty much 80% of what you do.
title: VRChat

notes:
Runs, but with substantial performance issues. Maps that operate at 90FPS locked on Windows routinely produce 40FPS or less given the same world instance and users/avatars present. If anyone has a solution to this, please post. Actively looking for one.

Also, as others have noted, get rid of the video player (youtube-dl.exe). It caused issues for me even in maps where there were (supposedly) no video players present.
title: VRChat

notes:
Can play just fine on desktop. However, entering certain worlds will freeze the game in black-screen, including popular ones like the Pug.  
Certain other worlds will prevent avatars from loading and prevent the game from transferring world downloads if you try to leave it.
Both cases require a relaunch.
title: VRChat

notes: It was possible to run VRChat in VR mode and in non-VR mode. Both run very smooth without that much of effort. The only thing I have is the Controller mapping. I am using the Valve Index Controller and if I use the thumbsticks in any way it enables the usage of ingame emotes (The green little hand icon) and for the Valve Index Controller you normally use the left thumbstick for moving around which means you automatically trigger the emote on and off.
title: VRChat
